title: Wedding Signage for Print (high res, not AI generated)

evidence: image 2 (current_drafts[0]) shows 'Welcome Maren & Elliot' with correct date — directly on-brief for Welcome Gathering Sign
image 5 (script_example) shows 'Olivia & James' — wrong couple name, serves only as style reference not a deliverable asset
deductions: image 3 (current_drafts[1]) is a seating chart template with placeholder 'Guest One/Two' names, not Maren & Elliot's actual guest data
No Welcome Ceremony & Reception Sign (35.4x23.6in landscape) asset exists in inventory at all
Existing signature drinks sign is provided as requested.
Both creative direction options have clear visual references.
All required content and print specifications are present.
Welcome sign and seating chart are generic, not Maren & Elliot, not correct palette/brand

fix.problem: Welcome sign is not for Maren & Elliot, not in required palette/brand, not correct copy
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate as 'Welcome Maren & Elliot Hartwell', use #FBF7EF, #EAC9C1, #9CAF88, #3B3B38, and Source Serif Pro/Lora fonts

evidence: wedding_copy.json and print_spec.json supply all copy and production specs for all 6 deliverable types
No image asset exists for Welcome Ceremony & Reception Sign (35.4x23.6in landscape) — a distinct sign type from the Gathering Sign
deductions: Welcome Ceremony & Reception Sign is a separately specified deliverable (different size, different wording) with zero reference or draft image supplied
seating_chart.json has 15 tables but the decision records only tables 1-6 get signs; the draft (image 3) shows up to 48 tables — structural mismatch an executor must resolve
All textual content for menu, program, welcome, and seating is present.
All print specifications (sizes, DPI, bleed) for deliverables are detailed.
Seating chart guest list is provided, fulfilling 'name lists soon' requirement.
No print-ready, high-res, editable template for any sign except signature drinks

fix.problem: Seating chart is generic, not Maren & Elliot, not correct guest list, not print-ready
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate as Maren & Elliot Hartwell seating chart, 15 tables, use provided guest_list, correct palette/fonts

evidence: image 2 (current_drafts[0]) is 1024x1536px — far below the 6000x9000px minimum the brief mandates; image_crop_and_resize cannot upscale to print quality
image 3 (current_drafts[1]) is 1024x1536px vs required 14160x10620px seating chart; fill_text step has no real names to merge into
deductions: All raster reference/draft images are ~1MP thumbnails; document_convert_pdf for large-format print requires source files at 300 DPI native resolution
Seating chart draft has placeholder text cells — fill_text tool needs identifiable text fields mapped to seating_chart.json rows, not lorem-ipsum blocks
All image files are standard formats for direct use or reference.
JSON data is well-structured for automated text filling and spec application.
No low-resolution AI-generated backgrounds are used for new design inputs.
Images are low-res (1024x1536), not suitable for print or cropping to spec

fix.problem: Not high-res, not vector/layered, not suitable for image_crop_and_resize or PDF export
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate at 6000x9000px, vector text, layered PSD/AI, correct copy and palette

evidence: image 1 (signature_drinks_sign) is a polished watercolour illustration — believable as a real client-created file, consistent with brief's 'I created' claim
image 2 (current_drafts[0]) is an AI-generated mockup on an easel — the brief explicitly states current backgrounds are AI-generated and flags this as the problem to fix
deductions: image 2 being an AI-generated scene mockup (easel, outdoor background) rather than a flat print-ready file confirms the exact quality problem the client hired to solve — it is the problem, not a usable input
image 3 seating chart with lorem-ipsum and non-sequential table numbers (13, 20, 23, 24…) would not come from a real client
Signature drinks sign is a believable custom wedding piece.
Floral and script references are authentic style examples.
JSON data contains realistic wedding details and guest lists.
Assets look like plausible drafts, but not real client handoff (wrong names, generic)

fix.problem: AI-generated scene mockup passed as a current draft; should be a flat design file showing the actual sign artwork without environmental staging
fix.prompt_delta: Render as a flat, white-background print-ready design file (no easel, no outdoor scene, no drop shadow). Show only the sign artwork at trim size with crop marks and bleed guides visible.

evidence: image 1 (signature_drinks_sign) uses ivory/blush/sage palette matching brand spec — strong anchor piece
image 2 (current_drafts[0]) uses pastel blue and lavender florals not in brand palette (#FBF7EF, #EAC9C1, #9CAF88, #3B3B38) — palette drift
deductions: image 2 floral colours (blue, lavender, peach) diverge from brand palette; new family must coordinate with image 1's blush/sage/ivory, not introduce new hues
image 3 seating chart uses cool blue-grey panel backgrounds inconsistent with ivory primary and charcoal ink secondary in brand spec
Signature drinks sign's style aligns with the brand's palette and voice.
Both floral and script references offer cohesive design directions for the brand.
Defined palette and fonts are consistently supported by visual assets.
Signature drinks sign is playful, others are generic and not in the same palette/brand

fix.problem: Not using brand fonts/colors, not visually matching signature drinks sign
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate with Source Serif Pro/Lora, #FBF7EF, #EAC9C1, #9CAF88, #3B3B38, and floral style

evidence: Decision to derive table signs from seating_chart.json tables 1-6 is sound — brief says 'I'll provide name lists soon' and the JSON now supplies them
Decision to treat floral_reference.jpg as stand-in for the linked arch-arrangement URL is reasonable given URL cannot be fetched by an agent
deductions: image 4 (floral_reference) is a real-photo arch arrangement, not the silk-flower product from the linked URL — the substitution is plausible but the style (lush garden roses vs silk) differs enough to risk wrong direction

Decision records 'primary direction = Option 1 soft floral' without client confirmation — client explicitly left both options open; committing to one without flagging it as an unresolved client choice is a risky assumption
Assumptions for program and table signs correctly identify them as layouts from data/specs.
Creative direction references are accurately mapped to client options.
The 'untouched' constraint for the signature drinks sign is correctly captured.
All assumptions are justified and documented

fix.problem: Real-photo garden-rose arch used as stand-in for silk-flower product reference; style gap could mislead floral illustration direction
fix.prompt_delta: Source or generate a reference image of a silk or watercolour-style floral arch arrangement featuring blush garden roses, white ranunculus, and sage eucalyptus — matching the soft, slightly stylised quality of the bifloralsilkflowers.com Emma arch product, not a photorealistic fresh-flower arrangement.

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): Every single source image is only 1024×1536 px — nowhere near the 6000×9000 px minimum I explicitly demanded for even the smallest sign. You cannot upscale a 1024px thumbnail to print-ready 300 DPI large-format and call it 'high res, not AI generated.' This entire handoff is built on low-res AI images dressed up with JSON specs.

AGENT CHOKE on 'current_drafts[1]': The image_crop_and_resize step will immediately fail or produce garbage when it tries to stretch a 1024×1536 px file to 14160×10620 px for the seating chart — a 9× upscale that destroys print quality and directly contradicts my core requirement of replacing AI-generated low-DPI backgrounds.
Completeness 6/10: Welcome Ceremony & Reception Sign is a separately specified deliverable (different size, different wording) with zero reference or draft image supplied
Executability 6/10: All raster reference/draft images are ~1MP thumbnails; document_convert_pdf for large-format print requires source files at 300 DPI native resolution
qc_gaps: Per-asset self-QC averages 9.2/10, but package Executability scored 6 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
Per-asset self-QC averages 9.2/10, but package Completeness scored 6 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
